doc_content.summary: The stark-rename-project skill performs a highly resilient, cross-repository project rename. It handles the GitHub API remote rename, safely updates git configurations, locally renames the project directory (including case-insensitive OS fallbacks), cleans up and reinstalls systemic symlinks, and performs deterministic and heuristically bound regex replacements within the renamed project and across sibling repositories. It features execution checkpoints to resume interrupted processes.
key_features: Resumable state detection comparing local and remote state
Perl-based literal regex replacement strictly isolating URL/repo boundaries
Lookaround-based heuristic boundaries preventing substring false positives
Orchestrated cross-repo auto-committing for sibling repos sharing origins
Strict phase-ordering ensuring symlinks are dropped before install.sh modifications

alt_text: A technical HTML visualization containing an execution flow diagram, data patterns grid, and resumable state table mapping out the stark-rename-project skill's internal architecture.
